title: Quercus × trabutiana
taxonomy: Quercus × trabutiana is a hybrid oak species. It is classified as a member of the family Fagaceae. The species is further classified under the genus Quercus, which encompasses a large number of oak species. Quercus × trabutiana is thought to be a hybrid between Quercus canariensis and Quercus faginea. The classification of this species is not universally agreed upon, and further research may be necessary to confirm its taxonomy.

intro: Quercus × trabutiana, also known as Trabut's oak, is a rare hybrid species of oak tree, resulting from the cross between Quercus petraea and Quercus robur. The bark and leaves of Quercus × trabutiana have been traditionally used in herbal medicine, offering several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. The bioactive constituents of Quercus × trabutiana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Quercus × trabutiana is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into herbal supplements and tinctures to support overall health and well-being.
